title: andropogon gerardi subsp. hallii
intro: Andropogon gerardi subsp. hallii, also known as buffalo grass, is a native North American grass species that belongs to the Poaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of buffalo grass include its ability to soothe wounds, reduce inflammation, and protect against infections, making it a valuable herb for wound care and skin issues. In herbal preparations, buffalo grass is often used in teas, tinctures, and salves to harness its medicinal properties and promote healing.
